title: Info Gain vs. Reward: Tuning the Knob

runtime.expected_outcome: Low preference_strength drives the agent to the longer info-rich path; high strength collapses it to the shortcut. Crossover is visible.

symbols.alpha, beta: implicit weights set by the scale of p(o) (C vector) and the agent's precision
symbols.preference_strength: the C scale; larger -> heavier pragmatic term

audiences.kid: If you're really hungry, you take the shortcut. If you're curious, you take the tour.
audiences.real: EFE's two terms compete. preference_strength is the main knob controlling the crossover between explore-and-exploit.
audiences.equation: G = epistemic + pragmatic; raise preference_strength to raise the pragmatic term's absolute scale.
audiences.derivation: Under softmax over -G at fixed precision, increasing the range of the pragmatic term reshapes the policy posterior toward preference-aligned options (Friston 2015).

orchestrate.objective: Find the preference_strength crossover between exploration and exploitation.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4.
